hypothesis: Replacing wholesale removal of cyclic training records with chronological loop erasure increases usable route and edge coverage while preserving simple positive-cost paths and one-record-one-sample weighting.

path_semantics.training: chronological loop erasure; at a repeated node remove the retained suffix and the loop-closing edge
path_semantics.development: drop cyclic records, exactly matching the frozen edge-only convergence study
path_semantics.confirmation: not used; the prior one-shot confirmation blocks remain sealed after their six frozen evaluations
path_semantics.weighting: each retained source record produces at most one unit-weight simple path
path_semantics.split_policy: deferred because unweighted flattening would over-represent cyclic source records

decision_rule: Report development relative regret first and F1/exact second at 20/50/100 prefixes. Treat a consistent 10-percent and full improvement as evidence to retain loop erasure for future training, but not as untouched confirmation evidence.
runtime_policy: Run 10 percent first and full serially; stop each process at 900 seconds; do not automatically add hyperparameter searches.
